verbGlossary.INITIATED AT: {source}record is first created and enters {target} at this open status, beginning the resolution lifecycle.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED VIA: {source} is routed to a responsible party through {target}.
verbGlossary.FOLLOWS: {source} adheres to this standard {target} pattern from creation through completion.
verbGlossary.ESCALATED VIA: {source} is automatically elevated to higher authority through this escalation {target} when severity warrants it.
verbGlossary.FLAGGED WHEN: {source} is marked with a warning indicator when it enters this overdue {target} without resolution.
verbGlossary.HELD DURING: {source} remains at this review {target} while subject matter experts evaluate the finding.
verbGlossary.ACCEPTED WITH: {source} is approved with conditions or annotations noted, acknowledging minor deviations from spec.
verbGlossary.AWAITS: {source} is queued and waiting for the next action at this {target}; no progress until someone acts.
verbGlossary.ROUTES THROUGH: {source} passes through this multi-step {target} pattern as part of its lifecycle processing.
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} lifecycle is controlled by this inspect-and-{target} pattern that defines its state transitions.
verbGlossary.QUEUED AT: {source} is staged and ready for a reviewer or inspector to pick up at {target}.
verbGlossary.PASSES TO: {source} receives a conforming {target}, indicating it meets the specified requirements.
verbGlossary.FAILS TO: {source} receives a deficient {target}, flagging it for corrective action before re-inspection.
verbGlossary.EXCLUDED TO: {source} is marked as not applicable to this scope, removing it from pass/fail tallies.
verbGlossary.DISPOSITIONED VIA: {source} conformance {target} is formally recorded through this workflow response mechanism.
verbGlossary.BOUND BY: {source}scheduled inspection is tied to a contractual or regulatory due date that cannot be missed.
verbGlossary.LAPSES TO: {source}scheduled inspection transitions to overdue {target} when it is not performed by its required date.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UPON: {source}inspection result is formally accepted and certified when it reaches approved {target}.
verbGlossary.CONDEMNED AT: {source}inspection result is rejected, meaning the tested element fails to meet code or specification requirements.
verbGlossary.CREATED AT: {source} is first logged at this open {target} during a walkthrough or final inspection.
verbGlossary.REMEDIATED DURING: {source} is being actively corrected by the responsible subcontractor while in this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.SIGNED OFF AT: {source} is formally accepted and closed after the project manager or owner verifies the fix.
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED BY: {source} lifecycle is organized according to this create-and-close {target} pattern for simple resolution tracking.
verbGlossary.AGED BY: {source} issue category's average time-to-close is measured using {target} dwell time metric.
verbGlossary.REINSPECTED AT: {source} resolution is staged for verification walkthrough at this ready-for-review {target}.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED UPON: {source} fix is confirmed acceptable and the item is formally approved for closeout.
verbGlossary.LOGGED AT: {source} is first recorded in the system at this open {target} immediately after the event occurs on site.
verbGlossary.INVESTIGATED DURING: {source} undergoes root cause analysis and documentation while in this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.FINALIZED AT: {source} record is closed after all investigations, corrective actions, and reporting are complete.
verbGlossary.ELEVATED VIA: {source} is automatically escalated to safety leadership through this escalation {target} based on severity.
verbGlossary.ORIGINATES IN: {source} is first created in this draft {target} as an editable document before formal submission.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED AT: {source} is formally submitted into the review queue at {target} for evaluation.
verbGlossary.COLLECTED AT: {source}is gathered and held at {target} until the investigation team processes it.
verbGlossary.ISSUED AT: {source} is formally created and assigned at this open {target} with a defined remediation scope.
verbGlossary.CLOSED UPON: {source} is verified complete and the record is closed, removing it from the active queue.
verbGlossary.EXPIRES TO: {source} transitions to overdue {target} when the responsible party misses the completion deadline.
verbGlossary.DELEGATED VIA: {source} responsibility is formally transferred to another party through this delegation {target}.
verbGlossary.CITED AT: {source} is formally issued and documented at this open {target}, beginning the enforcement workflow.
verbGlossary.NOTIFIED VIA: {source} {target}s notifications to all affected parties through this distribution list mechanism.
verbGlossary.DRAFTED IN: {source} report is initially composed in this draft {target} before engineering review.
verbGlossary.RETURNED FOR: {source} report is sent back for additional information or revised corrective action proposals.
verbGlossary.DISPOSITIONED UPON: {source} receives its formal engineering disposition (use-as-is, repair, reject, rework) at {target}.
verbGlossary.SERVED AT: {source} notice is formally delivered to the responsible party at this open {target}, starting the cure period.
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} notice's urgency is evaluated against this response time requirement to ensure timely correction.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED DURING: {source}issue is being actively worked between trades while in this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTED VIA: {source}issue is sent to multiple discipline leads simultaneously through this parallel review pattern.
verbGlossary.REDESIGNED VIA: {source}issue {target}s a revise-and-resubmit cycle for the affected drawing or specification.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source}issue initiates a change event lifecycle when the resolution requires a scope or cost change.
verbGlossary.FURNISHED AT: {source}test report is delivered to the project team at this submitted {target} for review against specifications.
verbGlossary.FAILED AT: {source}test report shows results outside specification limits and is rejected, requiring re-testing or material replacement.
verbGlossary.VALIDATED TO: {source}performance test confirms the installation meets the commissioning specification at this conforming {target}.
verbGlossary.PROCESSED VIA: {source}performance test follows this submit-and-approve {target} pattern for formal acceptance.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED UPON: {source}plan receives formal management {target}, committing resources and timeline to the corrective effort.
verbGlossary.SANCTIONED VIA: {source} is approved through this multi-level {target} chain involving safety and operations leadership.
verbGlossary.TRACKED DURING: {source}plan task is monitored for progress while in this active {target}.
verbGlossary.OWNED VIA: {source} is assigned to a specific responsible party through {target} ownership mechanism.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED AGAINST: {source} density metric is compared against {target} completion rate to assess closeout efficiency.
verbGlossary.COMPUTED AT: {source}cost percentage is calculated at this financial {target} stage as actual rework costs are recognized against the budget.
verbGlossary.DERIVED FROM: {source} pass rate metric is calculated from the outcomes produced by this inspect-and-{target} pattern.
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} closure rate is quantified using {target} aging metric to identify bottlenecks in resolution.
